mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and reduces recession risk pricing, so the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns when policy remains accommodative rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered non-tightening periods do not deliver higher 20-day forward returns than tightening periods, or if the sign of the FEDFUNDS effect is negative in a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates sharply, real yields and expected policy tightening rise, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the negative return spread versus non-triggered periods is not statistically distinguishable.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS indicates broad labor-market deterioration, which weakens household income expectations and lowers near-term equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months and PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-deterioration trigger does not outperform each single proxy alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not negative on average in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in RSAFS signals weakening retail demand and household spending momentum, which reduces expected revenue growth and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained RSAFS declines are followed by positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the return spread versus stable/expanding retail sales periods is negligible across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative INDPRO shock signals industrial output contraction and tighter goods supply capacity, which weakens cyclical earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon losses do not concentrate after INDPRO downside breaks, or if 5-day forward returns are not significantly below baseline after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound in HOUST signals improving construction demand and stronger cyclical activity, which supports equity returns over the next 60 trading days through higher growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-upturn triggers do not lead to positive 60-day returns versus baseline, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broad GDP trend proxies.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS reflects broad final-demand acceleration, and the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days as revenue and earnings expectations improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-retail demand state is not more predictive than either series alone, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive when both proxies improve concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and a low but rising FEDFUNDS rate reflects policy lag behind inflation, and this configuration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because real rates stay compressed while tightening risk rises.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS rises from its prior 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-and-policy trigger does not yield negative 60-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term loses sign consistency in rolling out-of-sample tests.
